dashboard.telemetryNotice: Anonymous telemetry is collected to improve Comfy Desktop.
dashboard.migrateBannerDesc: Your Legacy Desktop instance was detected. Migrate to a Standalone instance for better performance, easier updates, and full Comfy Desktop management.

confirmStopLocal.title: Return to Dashboard?
confirmStopLocal.message: This will stop the current ComfyUI.

chooser.title: Choose an instance
chooser.emptyDesc: Create your first ComfyUI instance to get started.
chooser.newInstallDesc: Set up a fresh ComfyUI environment.
chooser.searchPlaceholder: Search for and open an instance

titleBar.installSettingsComingSoon: Instance-specific settings will appear here in a future update.
titleBar.downloadsInProgress: no downloads in progress | {n} download in progress | {n} downloads in progress
titleBar.downloadsCompleteUnseen: no recent downloads | {n} download finished — click to review | {n} downloads finished — click to review
titleBar.downloadsFailedUnseen: no downloads failed | {n} download failed — click to review | {n} downloads failed — click to review
titleBar.pillHintBody: Click here to switch instances, start a new local instance, or return to the dashboard.

comfyUISettings.title: Settings
comfyUISettings.emptyInstallLess: Open a ComfyUI instance to view its settings.
comfyUISettings.storageGlobalNote: Changes here apply to all of your ComfyUI instances.
comfyUISettings.storageRestartNote: Restart the application (or close and reopen) for these changes to take effect.
comfyUISettings.extraModelPathsModalNote: Read-only — ComfyUI searches these directories, but the launcher does not manage them. Edit extra_model_paths.yaml to change them.

comfyLifecycle.crashedDesc: The ComfyUI process exited. You can restart it below.
comfyLifecycle.crashedDescWithCode: The ComfyUI process exited (exit code {code}). You can restart it below.
comfyLifecycle.crashedDescWithSignal: The ComfyUI process was terminated by {signal}. You can restart it below.
comfyLifecycle.crashedDescWithCodeAndSignal: The ComfyUI process was terminated by {signal} (exit code {code}). You can restart it below.
comfyLifecycle.crashedDescWithCodeHex: The ComfyUI process exited (exit code {code} / {hex}). You can restart it below.
comfyLifecycle.crashedDescAccessViolation: ComfyUI crashed with a memory access violation (exit code {code} / {hex}). This is usually a faulty or missing native library — not a ComfyUI bug — often surfacing while a Python package loads on startup. You can restart it below.
comfyLifecycle.crashedDescVcRuntimeHint: Some Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable runtime files appear to be missing; repairing or installing the latest redistributable may fix this.
comfyLifecycle.stoppedDesc: The ComfyUI server is stopped. This window stayed open — relaunch it below whenever you're ready.

models.directoriesDesc: Model directories shared across all instances. Models placed here are available to every ComfyUI instance, avoiding duplicate downloads. Newly downloaded models are saved to the Downloads directory.
models.lockedDir: This directory is always used and cannot be removed.
models.removeInstanceDirConfirm: This won't delete any files. You can re-add the directory later from this list.
models.removeDirConfirm: This won't delete any files. You can re-add the directory later from this list.

common.name: Name
common.portConflictAuto: Use next available port (asks if --port is set)

list.snapshotHardwareMismatch: The snapshot was created with {snapshotDevice}. Your detected hardware is {detectedDevice}. The recommended variant for your hardware has been pre-selected.

quickInstall.title: Install ComfyUI
quickInstall.grandSubtitle: Install the latest version of ComfyUI for your detected hardware.
quickInstall.desc: We'll install the latest version of ComfyUI. Please confirm your GPU type below.

loadSnapshot.grandSubtitle: Load a saved snapshot file to recreate that instance here.

newInstall.title: New Instance
newInstall.grandSubtitle: Set up a fresh ComfyUI instance. Pick a source, name it, and configure it.
newInstall.nvidiaDriverWarning: Your NVIDIA driver ({driverVersion}) may be too old for the bundled PyTorch. Recommended minimum: {minimumVersion}. Please consider updating your NVIDIA drivers if you run into issues.
newInstall.configureLead: How would you like this version installed?
newInstall.notAvailableRemote: Not available in Remote Connection mode
newInstall.helpImproveHint: Share anonymous usage data so we can fix bugs and prioritize features.
newInstall.nameWhitespace: Enter a name, or leave blank to use the suggested one.

templateQuit.title: Template models are still downloading
templateQuit.message: Quitting now will stop the download — it won't resume. You can let it finish, then quit.

diskSpace.warningMessage: The target drive only has {free} free. This operation may require approximately {required}. You may run out of space during the process.
diskSpace.warningMessageGeneric: The target drive only has {free} free. You may run out of space during the process.
diskSpace.templateBlockTitle: Not Enough Space for Template Models
diskSpace.templateBlockMessage: Needs ~{required}, only {free} free. Free up space or pick a lighter workflow.

pathValidation.insideAppBundleMessage: This path is inside the app's installation directory. Files here will be deleted when the app is updated. Please choose a different location.
pathValidation.oneDriveMessage: This path is inside OneDrive. Cloud sync can cause performance issues and file corruption with large Python environments. Please choose a location outside OneDrive.
pathValidation.insideSharedDirMessage: This path is inside a shared models, input, or output directory. Installing here can corrupt shared data. Please choose a different location.
pathValidation.insideExistingInstallMessage: This path overlaps with an existing instance. Installing here can cause conflicts and data corruption. Please choose a different location.

channelCards.noInfo: Select this channel and check for updates to see version info.

track.title: Add Existing Instance
track.grandSubtitle: Add an existing local ComfyUI checkout to Desktop without reinstalling it.

phaseLabel.restore-nodes: Restoring custom nodes from your snapshot…
phaseLabel.restore-pip: Reinstalling Python packages from your snapshot…
phaseLabel.comfy-update: Updating ComfyUI to the latest stable release…

console.title: ComfyUI Console
console.sessionEnded: This console session ended. Restart it to keep using the shell.

settings.title: Settings
settings.closeQuitMessage: The following are still active. Quitting will stop all of them.
settings.autoLaunchOnStartupDescription: Pick an instance to launch automatically when Comfy Desktop opens. Defaults to None.
settings.confirmBeforeClosingWindowDescription: Ask for confirmation before closing a window that's running a local install.
settings.hideCloudFromPicker: Hide Cloud from the Instance Picker
settings.hideCloudFromPickerDescription: For local-only users — hides the Cloud row from the Dashboard. Cloud remains fully functional; you can re-enable this any time.
settings.chineseMirrorsDescription: Git repositories clone from gitcode.com instead of github.com. PyPI packages are fetched from mirrors.aliyun.com and mirrors.cloud.tencent.com instead of pypi.org.
settings.chineseMirrorsSuggestMessage: Your system language appears to be Chinese. If you are located in China, enabling Chinese mirrors will significantly speed up downloads by using local mirror servers instead of github.com and pypi.org.

update.upToDate: You are running the latest version of Comfy Desktop.
update.debUpToDate: You are running the latest version. Updates for .deb installs are delivered through your system package manager (apt).

appUpdate.readyMessage: A Desktop update ({version}) has been downloaded. Restart now to apply it?
appUpdate.availableMessage: A Desktop update ({version}) is available. Download it now? You will be asked to restart once the download finishes.
appUpdate.panelIdleTitle: Comfy Desktop is up to date
appUpdate.systemManagedNote: Updates for this install are delivered through your system package manager.

overlay.cancelMessage: The operation will stop where it is. Anything not yet completed will be rolled back where possible.
overlay.quitSetupMessage: If you close now, your selection won't be saved and setup will start again the next time you launch Comfy Desktop.
overlay.discardSetupMessage: If you close now, your wizard selections won't be saved. No install has been created yet, so nothing will be rolled back.

firstUse.title: Welcome to Comfy Desktop
firstUse.grandSubtitle: A quick first-run setup so you're ready to launch ComfyUI.
firstUse.consentLead: Before you get started, please review the privacy policy below and choose whether to share anonymous usage telemetry.
firstUse.telemetryHint: Telemetry helps us improve the launcher. We never collect workflow contents, prompts, generated media, or personal information.
firstUse.consentTelemetryHint: Help improve Comfy by sharing anonymous usage data.
firstUse.expressInstallHint: Use recommended settings — skip optional setup steps.
firstUse.expressGpuHintSuffix: - if that's not your hardware, uncheck Express
firstUse.pickTitle: How do you want to run Comfy?
firstUse.pickLead: Choose the setup that fits how you work. You can switch later.
firstUse.cloudDesc: Best for **most users** who want to work from anywhere with models verified for commercial license.
firstUse.localDesc: Best for **power users** who want to manage versions, custom nodes, and offline workflows.
firstUse.migrateExistingDesc: Bring over **workflows, models, and settings** from your existing ComfyUI instance.

whyCloud.title: Why try Cloud?
whyCloud.lead: Your hardware has limits. Cloud doesn't.
benefits: Always up to date, nothing to maintain
Models ready to use, verified for commercial license

firstUse.localBranchTitle: It looks like you’ve installed Comfy before
firstUse.localBranchLead: How would you like this version installed?
firstUse.localBranchMigrateDesc: Bring over existing workflows, models, and settings.
firstUse.localBranchInstallNewDesc: Clean install. Leave existing projects in Comfy-{'{'}Name{'}'}.
firstUse.localDescRecommended: Fast install with **recommended settings** — skips optional setup steps.
firstUse.localDescRecommendedGpu: Fast install tuned for **{gpu}** with recommended settings. Pick **Configure** if that's not your hardware.

actions.restartConfirmMessage: This will stop ComfyUI and start it again. Any unsaved work will be lost.
actions.stopConfirmMessage: This will stop ComfyUI. Any unsaved work will be lost. The window stays open so you can relaunch anytime.
actions.featureNotImplemented: This feature is not yet implemented.
actions.copyInstallationMessage: Enter a name for the duplicate. It will be created next to the original.
actions.deleteConfirmMessage: This permanently removes this ComfyUI installation and all its files. This cannot be undone.
actions.untrackConfirmMessage: This will remove the instance from the app. The files will not be deleted.
actions.renameMessage: Enter a new name for this instance.

migrate.selectSourceMessage: Select a source instance to migrate from.
migrate.confirmMessage:
Select what to migrate from the source instance.

Custom nodes are copied additively (existing nodes backed up). Models, input, and output use flat merge (existing files are skipped).
migrate.noInstallations: No other instances available to migrate from.
migrate.noComfyUIDir: Source instance has no ComfyUI directory.
migrate.noUvOrPython: ⚠ Cannot install dependencies: uv or Python environment not found.
migrate.migrateToStandaloneConfirmMessage:
This will create a new Standalone instance with your custom nodes, workflows, settings, and user data. Your current instance will not be modified.

Models will be added to shared paths (not copied).
migrate.enablePipSync: Advanced: sync all pip packages (slower, usually not needed)

git.label: Git Clone
git.desc: Track an existing ComfyUI git clone with a Python virtual environment.
git.gitNotFound: Could not find git. Please install git and try again.
git.noVenv: No virtual environment configured. Cannot launch.
git.noMainPy: Cannot find ComfyUI main.py in this directory.

remote.label: Remote Connection
remote.desc: Connect to a running ComfyUI instance on another machine.

cloud.label: Cloud

signInBanner.message: We opened your browser to sign in. Didn’t open?

cloud.capacityDegradedHint: ComfyUI Cloud is under heavy load right now — you may see slower response times. Please try again later for the best experience.
cloud.capacityDisabledHint: ComfyUI Cloud is temporarily unavailable due to high demand. Please try again later.

desktop.label: Legacy Desktop
desktop.migrateConfirmMessage: This will create a new Standalone instance with your Legacy Desktop custom nodes, workflows, and settings. Your Legacy Desktop instance will not be modified.
desktop.reuseUserData: Reuse your existing workflows and settings in place
desktop.reuseInput: Reuse your existing input files in place
desktop.reuseOutput: Reuse your existing output files in place
desktop.addModels: Add Legacy Desktop models to shared paths
desktop.migrateBeforeLaunchMessage: Legacy ComfyUI Desktop must be migrated to Comfy Desktop before it can launch. We'll adopt your existing data, Python environment, and models in place, then start ComfyUI.
desktop.adoptConfirmMessage: Reuses your existing data folder, Python environment, and models in place. ComfyUI source code is staged into a new managed directory so updates work normally. Your Legacy Desktop instance is not modified.
desktop.adoptPromptTccMessage: macOS blocked access to your Legacy Desktop folder. Grant Full Disk Access in System Settings → Privacy & Security and retry.
desktop.adoptPromptVenvBrokenMessage: The Legacy Desktop Python environment failed to import torch. You can adopt anyway or cancel.
desktop.adoptPromptSourceMissingMessage: Couldn't obtain the ComfyUI source code needed to finish migrating your Legacy Desktop install. Retry, or cancel and create a new install instead.
desktop.adoptSourceMissingFailed: Couldn't obtain the ComfyUI source code needed to migrate your Legacy Desktop install. Check your internet connection and try again. If this keeps happening, create a new install from the dashboard instead.

standalone._note: standalone.label and portable.label are product names — keep in English across all translations.
standalone.label: Standalone
standalone.desc: Pre-built environment with managed Python and dependencies.
standalone.templatePickerLead: Opens a ready-to-run workflow. Models download during setup.
standalone.templateSkipAndInstallAria: Skip starter workflow and install ComfyUI
standalone.templateDontShowAgain: Don't show this template step again
standalone.templateModelsError: Couldn't download some models — you can grab them in‑app
standalone.templateModelsNoSpace: Not enough disk space for the template models — free up space and grab them in‑app
standalone.templateModelsFailedCountdown: Model download failed — you can retry it in‑app. Opening ComfyUI in {secs}…
standalone.newEnvMessage: Enter a name for the new environment.
standalone.cannotDeleteActive: Cannot delete the active environment. Set another environment as active first.
standalone.deleteEnvConfirmMessage: Delete environment "{env}"? This cannot be undone.
standalone.cleanupEnvStatus: Removing redundant packages from template environment…
standalone.channelStableDesc: Tested releases, recommended for most users
standalone.channelLatestDesc: Newest commits from the master branch
standalone.upToDateMessage: No updates available — you're already up to date.
standalone.updateConfirmMessageLatest: Update {installed} to the latest master commit ({latest})?
standalone.updateConfirmMessageDowngrade: Roll back from {installed} to stable {latest}? Newer commits will be removed.
standalone.updateBreakingWarning: ⚠️ This may break your custom nodes and workflows.
standalone.updateFailed: Update process failed with exit code {code}.
standalone.updateNoGit: No .git directory found in ComfyUI/. Commit-based updating requires a git repository.
standalone.copyAndUpdateMessage: Copy this instance, then update the copy from {installed} to {latest}. The original is untouched.
standalone.copyAndUpdatePlaceholder: Enter a name for the copy
standalone.releaseUpdateSelectReleaseMessage: Choose a Standalone release to install.
standalone.releaseUpdateSelectVariantMessage: Choose an environment variant for the new instance.
standalone.releaseUpdateNameMessage: Enter a name for the new instance. It will be created next to the original.
standalone.snapshotHistoryEmpty: No snapshots yet. Snapshots are captured automatically when ComfyUI starts.
standalone.snapshotCreateMessage: Create a snapshot of the current environment state. Optionally provide a label.
standalone.snapshotDeleteMessage: Delete this snapshot? This cannot be undone.
standalone.snapshotRestoreMessage: Restore nodes and packages to match this snapshot? A backup is made first.
standalone.snapshotRestoreStopRequired: Stop ComfyUI before restoring a snapshot.
standalone.snapshotRestoreReverted: Pip restore failed — reverted to pre-restore state
standalone.snapshotRestoreNothingToDo: No changes needed — environment already matches snapshot.
standalone.snapshotDiffNoChanges: No differences — environment matches this snapshot.

snapshots.empty: No snapshots yet. Snapshots are captured automatically when ComfyUI starts.
snapshots.noSnapshotsToShare: There are no snapshots to share yet.
snapshots.noPrevious: First snapshot — no previous to compare
snapshots.deleteConfirmMessage: Delete this snapshot? Your instance and other snapshots are unaffected.
snapshots.restoreConfirmMessage: Replace the current instance state with this snapshot? A backup is made first.
snapshots.importConfirmMessage: These snapshots will be added to this instance. The newest one will replace your current state.
snapshots.restorePreviewTitle: Restore Preview — changes that will be applied:
snapshots.restoreNoChanges: No changes needed — environment already matches this snapshot.
snapshots.restoreConfirm: Are you sure you want to restore this snapshot? Your current instance state will be replaced.
snapshots.importAlreadyCurrent: This snapshot already matches the current state.

portable.label: Portable
portable.desc: Official portable Windows builds with embedded Python.
portable.channelStableDesc: Tested releases, recommended for most users
portable.channelLatestDesc: Newest commits from the master branch
portable.updateConfirmMessageLatest: Update {installed} to the latest master commit ({latest})?
portable.updateFailed: Update process failed with exit code {code}.
portable.noUpdateDir: No update directory found in this portable install.

installer.waitingForDownload: Waiting for another installation to finish downloading…

launch.waiting: Waiting for ComfyUI to be ready…
launch.waitingTime: Waiting for ComfyUI to be ready… ({secs}s)
launch.portBusyUsing: Port {old} is busy — using port {new} instead…
launch.instanceRunningMessage: Choosing "Close & Launch" will stop the instances listed below.
launch.modelFolderRelaunchDesc: New model folders were detected from custom nodes. Restarting so ComfyUI can use them right away.
launch.updateInstallDesc: A new version was downloaded and is about to install. The app will close briefly and reopen automatically.

errors.noLaunchSupport: This source does not support launching yet.
errors.noEnvFound: Cannot launch: no Python environment found. Create one first.
errors.alreadyRunning: ComfyUI is already running. Stop it before launching again.
errors.installDirEmpty: Instance folder is missing or empty. Delete this instance and reinstall.
errors.installNotReady: This instance is not ready to launch. It may still be installing or may have failed.
errors.deleteLocked:
A file could not be deleted because it is in use by another program. Close any applications that may have files open in this directory and try again.
{path}
errors.deleteLockedBy:
A file could not be deleted because it is in use by: {processes}. Close these applications and try again.
{path}
errors.cannotConnect: Could not connect to {url}. Make sure ComfyUI is running at that address.
errors.portConflictLauncher: Port {port} is already in use by "{name}", launched from another Comfy Desktop instance. You can use the next available port, stop it first, or change the port in Startup Arguments.
errors.portConflictComfy: Port {port} is already in use by {process}, which appears to be a ComfyUI instance. You can use the next available port, stop the process, or change the port in Startup Arguments.
errors.portConflictOther: Port {port} is already in use by {process}. You can use the next available port or change the port in Startup Arguments.
errors.portConflictKillConfirmMessage: This will forcefully stop the process currently using this port. Any unsaved work in that ComfyUI instance will be lost.
errors.portConflictKillFailed: Could not free port {port}. The process may require manual termination.
errors.duplicateName: An instance named "{name}" already exists. Please choose a different name.
errors.unknownSource: This instance has an unrecognized source type. It may have been created by a different version. Delete it and reinstall.
errors.operationInProgress: "{operation}" is currently in progress for this instance. Would you like to cancel it?
errors.operationInProgressGeneric: Another operation is currently in progress for this instance. Would you like to cancel it?
errors.stopRequired: This instance is currently running and must be stopped first.
errors.willStopRunning: {name} is running and will be stopped first.
errors.folderPermissionDenied: macOS denied access to "{path}". Please open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Files and Folders, grant access to Comfy Desktop, then retry.

tooltips.instances: A separate ComfyUI instance with its own version, models, and settings.
tooltips.snapshots: A saved point-in-time state of an instance (versions + custom nodes) you can restore later.
tooltips.console: An interactive shell running in this instance's folder. Works whether ComfyUI is running or stopped.
tooltips.tabUpdate: Check for and install updates to this ComfyUI instance. Pick a release channel and review release notes before updating.
tooltips.tabConfig: Command-line arguments passed to ComfyUI on launch — port, low-VRAM mode, custom directories, and other startup overrides.
tooltips.tabStorage: Disk usage for this instance and the shared model, input, and output directories it reads from.
tooltips.tabStatus: Instance details — version, install location, last launched, source, and other metadata about this instance.
tooltips.standalone: A self-contained instance with its own bundled Python and dependencies. Easy to update and manage.
tooltips.portable: The official portable Windows build with embedded Python. Runs from a single folder without a system-wide install.
tooltips.git: A git-based install cloned from the ComfyUI repository. Requires git and Python to be installed on your system.
tooltips.snapshot: A saved record of your environment's state — ComfyUI version, custom nodes, and pip packages — that can be restored later.
tooltips.updateChannel: Controls where updates come from. 'Stable' uses tested releases; 'Latest' pulls the newest commits directly from GitHub.
tooltips.startupArgs: Command-line arguments passed to ComfyUI when it starts, such as --port or --lowvram.
tooltips.launchMode: Choose how ComfyUI runs: in its own app window, or as a console-only process.
tooltips.browserPartition: Controls whether this instance shares browser cache (cookies, local storage) with other instances or keeps its own separate cache.
tooltips.sharedDirs: Directories shared across all instances for inputs and outputs.
tooltips.sharedModels: Folders shared across all instances so models aren't downloaded twice. Newly downloaded models go to the Downloads folder. The system default folder is always kept and can't be removed, and the Downloads folder can't be removed while it's in use — pick a different one first.
tooltips.modelsPrimary: Newly downloaded models are saved here.
tooltips.instanceOwnModelsDir: This instance's own models folder. It's always used and is never shared with other instances.
tooltips.sharedDir: Shared across all your ComfyUI instances.
tooltips.extraModelPathsInstance: A custom model path from this instance's extra_model_paths.yaml. ComfyUI searches it; the launcher shows it read-only. Click for details.
tooltips.extraModelPathsDefault: This base path is marked is_default in extra_model_paths.yaml, so ComfyUI prefers it for those model types. For shared model types the launcher's Downloads directory is loaded later and takes precedence.
tooltips.modelsDefault: The system default directory. This path is created automatically and cannot be removed.
tooltips.useSharedModels: When enabled, this instance can see every model in your global shared model folders. Disabling hides those models — most users want this on.
tooltips.useSharedInputOutput: When enabled, this instance reads and writes input and output files from the global shared folders. Disable to point this instance at its own folders below.
tooltips.perInstallModelDirs: Extra model directories for this instance only, used while shared models is off. The first directory is where newly downloaded models are saved.
tooltips.perInstallInputDir: Folder this instance reads input files from. Leave blank to use this instance's own internal input folder.
tooltips.perInstallOutputDir: Folder this instance writes generated output files to. Leave blank to use this instance's own internal output folder.
tooltips.inputDir: Shared folder for input files (images, masks, etc.) accessible to all instances.
tooltips.outputDir: Shared folder where ComfyUI saves generated outputs, accessible to all instances.
tooltips.installDir: Default parent folder suggested when creating new instances. Existing instances aren't moved.
tooltips.autoDownloadOutputs: Automatically download output files (images, videos) from this remote instance to a local directory after each workflow run.
tooltips.useSharedOutputDir: When enabled, downloaded outputs are saved to the shared output directory. Disable to use a custom output folder for this instance.
tooltips.outputDirPerInstall: Custom local folder where outputs from this remote instance will be saved.
tooltips.customNodes: Third-party extensions that add new node types and functionality to ComfyUI.
tooltips.pipPackages: Python packages installed in the environment. Custom nodes often install their own pip dependencies.
tooltips.copyAndUpdate: Copy this instance and update the copy. The original is untouched.
tooltips.envVars: Custom environment variables passed to ComfyUI when it starts. Warning: values are stored unencrypted in the configuration file — do not add API keys, tokens, or other sensitive information.

envVars.name: Name
envVars.securityWarning: Environment variable values are stored unencrypted on disk. Do not add API keys, tokens, passwords, or other secrets.

instancePicker.openDashboardHint: Opens the dashboard in a new window. Your running instance keeps running.
instancePicker.restartConfirmDetail: Restarting will stop the running session. Any unsaved work in the workflow will be lost.
